CMake toolchain file for configuring STM32F3 microcontroller with ARM GCC compiler.

# Purpose
This configuration file is used to set up the build environment for a project targeting a specific microcontroller unit (MCU) architecture. It specifies the system name as `MCU`, the processor as `armv7l`, and the system version as `STM32F3`, which indicates the target hardware platform. The file defines common architecture flags under `FLAGS_COMMON_ARCH` to configure the compiler for the ARM Cortex-M4 architecture, including options for little-endian format, thumb instruction set, and hardware floating-point support. The `CMAKE_FORCE_C_COMPILER` command enforces the use of the `arm-none-eabi-gcc` compiler, which is a GNU compiler for ARM embedded processors. This setup ensures that the build process is tailored for the specified ARM architecture and processor.
